_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of human behavior and performance; individual differences in ability, personality, and interests; learning and motivation; psychological research methods; and the assessment and treatment of behavioral and affective disorders.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of change management frameworks and organizational behavior principles relevant to data and AI transformations, including resistance patterns, adoption drivers, stakeholder engagement models, and cultural assessment methods.
comments: Partial overlap: organizational psychology, behavior, and motivation concepts underpin WIDAI change management and leadership KSAs. O*NET covers broad psychology including clinical. WIDAI LS-K-009 covers organizational behavior subset. Secondary: LS-S-005 (stakeholder engagement), AG-K-009 (AI literacy — learning/cognition).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score reflects a genuine but bounded intersection. The overlapping concepts are detectable but represent a subset of what each description covers independently.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8643 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Contradiction-dominant (99.97%) — the model detects tension between the descriptions. A strong contradiction signal is unusual for a mapped pair and may indicate the descriptions address the same domain from opposing perspectives, or that the NLI model is reacting to scope/specificity differences rather than actual semantic conflict. This warrants attention during human review.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3168) is moderately lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.4202). The gap (0.10) indicates the relationship has a cross-abstraction component — some of the semantic alignment requires joint processing to detect, suggesting vocabulary or scope divergence between the two descriptions.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (14.29% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 100.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
